Job.JobTitle: Head Coach Unified Track and Field
Job.JobDescription:
Hempfield School District is looking for a Track & Field Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Unified Track and Field Team Head Coach Hempfield High School Job Summary: Oversee the administration of the high school's Unified Track and Field team which is composed of a proportional number of students who are Special Olympics-eligible (defined as students with an intellectual disability, cognitive delay, or developmental disability) and students without intellectual disabilities. Position Information: Part-time, no benefits 2025-2026 Salary - $600 2 practices per week, 1 home meet, and 2 away meets with an estimated 40 hours for the season Roles & Responsibilities: Liaison regularly with the school's athletic director and Special Olympics Pennsylvania to ensure quality program standards are being met. Develop and implement practice plans in which track and field instruction and coaching is provided to all participants. Impart the philosophy of a positive inclusive sports experience by following the principles of Unified Sports®. Provide guidance and manage team strategies at Unified Track and Field competitions. Recruit a proportional number of students who are Special Olympics-eligible. Handle any paperwork or administrative duties required by the school district and/or Special Olympics Pennsylvania. If serving as a host site for a Unified Track and Field competition, recruit volunteers to help conduct and manage the events. Attend the free Unified Sports® Track and Field Coaches' Training in your county. Complete the free online Special Olympics coaches' courses. Qualifications: Coaching experience preferred Evidence of effective leadership Ability to articulate vision for building/enhancing the scholastic program Excellent communication and problem solving skills Required Documents: Submission of a pre-employment medical examination Current PA Criminal History background check Current Federal Criminal background check Current Child Abuse background check Interested candidates should submit an online application. Questions can be directed to hroffice@hempfieldsd.org . EOE

Position Title: Band (Pit) Instructor Location: TBD Position Summary/Goal: To provide an educational atmosphere in which students will move toward the fulfillment of their potential for intellectual, emotional, physical and psychological growth. Term of Employment: 12 Month Position Reporting Relationships: Reports to Marching Band Director/Athletic Director Qualifications: Possess the ability to effectively advise and instruct middle through high school aged students in the skills of pit percussion instruments. Possess the ability to instruct percussion instruments with proficiency including, but not limited to the following: xylophone, marimba, bells, vibraphone, chimes, and auxiliary percussion. Have the knowledge and ability to manage and instruct the use of a sound board and system as well as electronics such as synthesizers, sample pad, etc. Possess the ability to work with band director, and other members of marching band staff to cooperate in the designing of competitive marching band program. Have a willingness to attend all rehearsals and functions as scheduled by the marching band director. Have the ability to identify and coach students of various developmental levels in order to insure individual success as performers. Demonstrate experience in competitive marching band (minimum 3 years). Collaborate with music instructors. Requirements: Strong communication, public relations, and interpersonal skills Must hold or obtain FBI, Criminal Check, and Child Abuse Clearances. Evaluation: Annual evaluation will be conducted by the Dean of VPA with input from the Marching Band Director. This list is intended to be illustrative rather than complete and serves to show major duties and responsibilities and does not express or imply that these are the only duties to be performed by the incumbent in this position. The employee will be required to perform any other position-related duties requested by the supervisorc. THE ERIE SCHOOL DISTRICT DOES NOT DISCRIMINATE IN EMPLOYMENT, EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS OR ACTIVITIES BASED ON AGE, RACE, COLOR, RELIGION, NATIONAL ORIGIN, SEX, DISABILITY OR BECAUSE A PERSON IS A DISABLED VETERAN OR VETERAN OF THE VIETNAM ERA. THIS POLICY OF NON-DISCRIMINATION EXTENDS TO ALL OTHER LEGALLY PROTECTED CLASSIFICATIONS. PUBLICATION OF THIS POLICY IS IN ACCORDANCE WITH STATE AND FEDERAL LAWS INCLUDING TITLE VII OF THE CIVIL RIGHTS ACT OF 1964, TITLE IX OF THE EDUCATION AMENDMENTS OF 1972, SECTION 504 OF THE REHABILITATION ACT OF 1973, AND THE AMERICANS WITH DISABILITIES ACT. INQUIRIES MAY BE MADE BY CONTACTING THE ERIE SCHOOL DISTRICT, 148 WEST 21ST STREET, ERIE PA 16502, (814) 874-6080.

Job.JobDescription:
Erie City School District is looking for a Football Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Football Equipment Manager High School and Middle School Positions Available: (1) Position Summary The Football Equipment Manager is responsible for the management, distribution, maintenance, inventory control, and safety compliance of all football equipment for the Erie School District Football Program (Varsity, Junior Varsity, and Middle School). This position plays a critical role in ensuring compliance with PIAA, NFHS, PDE, and NOCSAE regulations and supporting athlete safety standards consistent with a PIAA program. Term of Employment 12-Month Position Location Erie High School Reporting Relationships Reports to the Head Football Coach Reports to the Director of Athletics Collaborates with Athletic Trainer and Building Administration Qualifications Experience managing athletic equipment, preferably football. Knowledge of PIAA, NFHS, and NOCSAE equipment standards. Understanding of proper helmet and shoulder pad fitting protocols. Strong organizational, inventory management, and documentation skills. Ability to lift, transport, and manage heavy equipment. Strong interpersonal and communication skills. Ability to work evenings, weekends, and extended seasonal hours. Urban student experience preferred. Required Clearances & Certifications Act 34  PA Criminal History Clearance Act 151  Child Abuse Clearance Act 114  FBI Fingerprint Clearance Mandated Reporter Training Concussion Awareness Certification and Sudden Cardiac (NFHS) Completion of all required district compliance trainings Essential Duties and Responsibilities Safety & Compliance Ensure all helmets and protective equipment meet NOCSAE standards and are annually reconditioned and certified. Maintain documentation of serial numbers, reconditioning dates, and compliance records. Remove damaged or non-compliant equipment from circulation immediately. Ensure adherence to all PIAA and NFHS equipment regulations. Support district concussion and emergency action protocols. Inventory & Equipment Operations Maintain a comprehensive inventory system for Varsity, JV, and Middle School football equipment. Supervise distribution, fitting, tracking, and collection of all equipment and uniforms. Conduct pre-season and post-season equipment audits. Maintain secure and organized equipment storage areas. Coordinate ordering and replacement of equipment in collaboration with the Head Coach and Athletic Director. Adhere to district purchasing and budget procedures. Game & Practice Responsibilities Attend all home football games (MS, JV, Varsity). Attend designated practices to manage equipment needs. Coordinate transport, setup, and breakdown of equipment for home and away contests. Off-Season Responsibilities Conduct post-season inventory and damage assessments. Coordinate helmet and pad reconditioning process. Develop equipment replacement plan aligned with safety standards. Participate in planning meetings with the Athletic Department. Professional Expectations Demonstrate professionalism and ethical conduct at all times. Maintain positive working relationships with coaches, student-athletes, and staff. Represent Erie's Public Schools in a professional and responsible manner. Evaluation Performance will be evaluated by the Head Football Coach and Director of Athletics in accordance with district policies and the Athletic Handbook. This description is intended to be illustrative rather than exhaustive. Additional duties may be assigned as needed. THE ERIE SCHOOL DISTRICT DOES NOT DISCRIMINATE IN EMPLOYMENT, EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS OR ACTIVITIES BASED ON AGE, RACE, COLOR, RELIGION, NATIONAL ORIGIN, SEX, DISABILITY OR BECAUSE A PERSON IS A DISABLED VETERAN OR VETERAN OF THE VIETNAM ERA. THIS POLICY OF NON-DISCRIMINATION EXTENDS TO ALL OTHER LEGALLY PROTECTED CLASSIFICATIONS. PUBLICATION OF THIS POLICY IS IN ACCORDANCE WITH STATE AND FEDERAL LAWS INCLUDING TITLE VII OF THE CIVIL RIGHTS ACT OF 1964, TITLE IX OF THE EDUCATION AMENDMENTS OF 1972, SECTION 504 OF THE REHABILITATION ACT OF 1973, AND THE AMERICANS WITH DISABILITIES ACT. INQUIRIES MAY BE MADE BY CONTACTING THE ERIE SCHOOL DISTRICT, 1910 Sassafras St, ERIE PA 16502, (814) 874-6080
Job.CompanyDescription: Erie City School District coaching jobs

Job.JobTitle: Head Coach Boys Junior Varsity Baseball
Job.JobDescription:
Erie City School District is looking for a Baseball Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
Head Coach Boys Junior Varsity Baseball Position Summary/Goal: The Head Coach will facilitate and enhance communication and collaboration between and among the Erie School District and parents, students, and community centers. The Head Coach will promote a positive school culture and foster academic excellence and exemplify good character for the individual athlete. Furthermore, the Erie School District expects the Head Coach to be a positive role model, a mentor to student-athletes, and embrace all concepts of good citizenship. Term of Employment: 12 month position, effective for the 2025-2026 School Year Schools/Location: Erie High School Reporting Relationships: High School Varsity Head Coach Reports to Director of Athletics May work on specific projects with program directors or building principals. Qualifications: Head coaching experience at the high school or middle school level, minimum of 3 years experience required. Evidence of leading a successful middle school or high school baseball program with a winning record and post season coaching experience. Ability to transition student athletes to the next competitive level (ability to articulate concrete examples and procedures regarding the transition of student athletes to the next competitive level). The ability to design offensive and defensive strategies. Experience in coaching offense and defense. Evidence of practice plans developed in prior coaching positions. Must hold or obtain FBI, Criminal Check, and Child Abuse Clearances. Working knowledge of Erie School District policies, procedures, and organizational structure. Knowledge of current trends, tactics, strategies and skills in baseball. Must possess strong interpersonal skills and communication skills. Ability to build positive relationships with urban student populations. Urban student experience a plus. Requirements: Must complete all Coaching Education requirements of the PIAA and PDE in a timely manner. Must adhere to all PIAA bylaws and eligibility standards. Must hold or obtain Act 34 (Criminal History), Act 151 (Child Abuse), and Act 114 (FBI Fingerprint) clearances. Completion of mandated reporter training. Completion of concussion management certification and other state required safety trainings. Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Instruct student-athletes in the fundamentals of baseball, including rules of the game, proper techniques, and skill development. Plan and carry out daily practice plans aligned with the varsity program philosophy. Supervise all practices, games, and team-related activities. Assist varsity coach with development of a structured feeder system throughout the baseball program. Promote, maintain, and ensure high academic standards for all athletes. Monitor academic progress and secure support when needed. Conduct pre-season and post-season conditioning programs in collaboration with district guidelines. Promote good sportsmanship and serve as a positive role model. Maintain positive communication with athletes, families, community members, and district administration. Ensure compliance with all PIAA, district, and state regulations. Develop and clearly communicate expectations for athlete participation and conduct. Assist with scheduling in coordination with the Athletic Director and Varsity Coach. Manage ordering, collection, distribution, and inventory of equipment and uniforms. Submit required paperwork, eligibility forms, and roster information in a timely manner. Maintain accurate records including attendance, statistics, and equipment logs. Professionalism: Must possess the ability to be a collaborative team player. Must be cooperative, courteous, service-oriented, and promote these characteristics within the team. Demonstrate professional conduct at all times with officials, opponents, parents, and community members. Represent Erie's Public Schools with the highest level of ethical behavior and professional standards. Off-Season Responsibilities: Attend off-season team camps and potential leagues. Organize off-season open gym/weight room sessions, practice plans, and instruction. Participate in youth outreach efforts to strengthen program sustainability. Other Duties: Attend staff meetings, clinics, and professional development activities to improve coaching performance. Participate in district-required professional development. Perform other related duties as assigned by the Head Baseball Coach, Athletic Director, Principal, or appropriate administrator. Evaluation: High School Boys Varsity Head Coach and Building Administrator This list is intended to be illustrative rather than complete and serves to show major duties and responsibilities and does not express or imply that these are the only duties to be performed by the incumbent in this position. The employee will be required to perform any other position-related duties requested by the supervisor.

Job.JobDescription:
Central Bucks School District is looking for a Baseball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION: Assistant Coach - 7th Grade Baseball LOCATION: Unami Middle School AVAILABLE: Spring 2026 COMPENSATION: Points DESCRIPTION: Unami Middle School is accepting applications for a 7th Grade Assistant baseball Coach. The 7th Grade Assistant Coach will work closely with the 7th Grade Coach to coordinate the program. The successful candidate will have knowledge of the sport, strong communication skills and be able to teach the fundamentals of baseball to middle school students. Candidate must understand the unique role of the student/athlete and have a strong commitment to values-based coaching. REQUIREMENTS: Previous playing or coaching experience preferred. PROCEDURE: Apply through the CBSD Employment Portal on or before April 11, 2026*. EOE *The district reserves the right to close this posting after seven (7) calendar days should a suitable applicant be found. Interested CBSD employees should apply through the INTERNAL JOB POSTINGS link on the CBSD INTRANET under Staff. All employment is provisional for 90 days as required by ACT 168 of 2014, enacted December 22, 2014. IF YOU ARE A NEW HIRE WITH CBSD , you must complete the ACT 168 INQUIRY FORM which is included in the New Hire Checklist. See details and instructions on the Inquiry Form.

Job.JobTitle: Special Education Program Specialist (Curriculum Support) Full-time/10-month position Contract Eligible (with proper PDE Certification*)
Job.JobDescription:
Central Bucks School District is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION: Special Education Program Specialist (Curriculum Support) Full-time/10-month position Contract Eligible (with proper PDE Certification*) LOCATION: ESC - Special Education Dept AVAILABLE: 2025/2026 School Year (Exact start date TBD) Full-time/10-month position w/ some Summer Work Required COMPENSATION: Salary in accordance with CBEA Contract DESCRIPTION: Self-directed professional with the knowledge and experience to work effectively in the field with learning support teachers, assess program quality and provide feedback to supervisors in order to sustain and develop high quality programs and practices. Design and implementation of staff-development sessions. Support teachers in program planning, IEP development, technology application and classroom management. Essential Duties and Responsibilities (List is not all-inclusive): 1. Teacher Support Coaching and Modeling of current instructional, social/emotional programs and lesson plan design with intensive support for new teachers Developing a framework for organization and management of the learning support classroom including effective use of para professionals Problem solving to meet the needs of the multiple learning profiles and social/emotional weaknesses of students within the present-day learning support programs Supporting the IEP process to include the development, implementation and progress monitoring of individualized educational programs Support technology implementation and application in order to support instructional program planning and participation in teaching-learning process. 2. Program Development Staying current in research based best practices for all students serviced in the learning support program Assessing and responding to needs within the instructional program Spearhead multi-year instructional initiatives Monitoring the effectiveness of current programs through data collection and analysis Coordinating and utilizing in district and out of district resources 3. Staff Development Provide staff development to special education teachers and para professionals based on Supervisor direction Provide training, support and feedback to teachers with Orton-Gillingham methodology and instructional practices Generate and provide staff development in programs and protocols that are standard to the general education and special education settings. REQUIREMENTS: Bachelor's degree in Special Education, Master's Degree in Education; PA teaching certification; 5 years teaching experience, Behavioral background. Ability to communicate effectively with colleagues both orally and in writing necessary. * If applicant requires a PDE Emergency Permit, contract eligibility is not applicable, and the vacancy becomes a Long Term Substitute position PROCEDURE: A pply through the CBSD Employment Portal on or before March 27, 2026*. EOE *The district reserves the right to close this posting after five (5) calendar days should a suitable applicant be found. Interested CBSD employees should apply through the INTERNAL JOB POSTINGS link on the CBSD INTRANET under Staff. All employment is provisional for 90 days as required by ACT 168 of 2014, enacted December 22, 2014. IF YOU ARE A NEW HIRE WITH CBSD, you must complete the ACT 168 INQUIRY FORM which is included in the New Hire Checklist. See details and instructions on the Inquiry Form.
Job.SalaryRange: Salary in accordance with CBEA Contract

Job.JobDescription:
Summary: Athletic positions require someone with proven decision-making skills, a track record of initiative and effort and a commitment to young people and education. Our jobs require a “people person” that indicates through actions a personality that is conducive to positive interactions with students, staff and parents.
To view the job description for each type of athletic coach, click here on the Athletics Position Descriptions. Click here on the Supplements for Athletics link to see athletics supplementary salary schedule.
Coaching positions require the following Continuing Education Requirements: - GCS Safety Videos Required Annually: - Bloodborne Pathogen Exposure Prevention - FERPA: Confidentiality of Records - GCS – Title IX & Safe Schools - GCS – Code of Ethics & Social Media - GCS – Information Security Mgmt System - GCS – Safety Training - GCS – Mandated Reporter - Greenville Energy Conservation - Hazard Communication: Right to Understand - Health Emergencies: Overview - Sexual Harassment: Staff to Staff - GCS – Active Shooter Training - NFHS Courses Required Annually: - Concussion in Sport - Heat Illness Prevention - Sudden Cardiac Arrest - Protecting Students from Abuse - NFHS Accredited Interscholastic Coach – Required Once for all New Head Coaches - Fundamentals of Coaching ($60) - Football Coaches - Heads UP Football Safety Coach Certification - Strength Coaches - NHSSCA – Must be certified to receive the strength supplement - Training Required in 1st Year then Every 2 Years: CPR – The GCS CPR certification process is offered in a blended learning format, which means you complete an online course FIRST, and then follow it with a hands-on skills evaluation session within a maximum of 60 days after online course completion.

Southside High is looking for a Football Assistant Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.

Beaufort County is looking for a Football Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION PURPOSE: Prepares each participating student athlete for competition in sports activities, and helps them to achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Prioritize athletics as a supplemental program to support the academic mission of the school. Carry out the aims and objectives of the sports program as outlined by the Athletic Director and school district administration. Instruct athletes in individual and team fundamentals, sportsmanship, cooperation, strategy and playing tactics, and physical conditioning necessary to realize individual and team success. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling intramural and interscholastic contests. Assist in planning and scheduling a regular program of practice in season. Maintain required attendance forms, insurance records, and other related documents. Assist in oversight of safety conditions in the facility or area in which the assigned sport is conducted at all times student athletes are present. Assist in establishing and implementing performance criteria for academic eligibility in interscholastic competition of the identified sport. Enforce code of conduct standards and sportsmanlike behavior at all times; establish and oversee penalties for breach of standards by student athletes. Recommend purchase of equipment, supplies, uniforms, etc. as appropriate. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Athletic Director, Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. Knowledge and/or background in the assigned sport. Teaching certificate and experience preferred. Must be able to pass criminal background check. Valid drivers license. EXPERIENCE : Must have knowledge of South Carolina High School League rules and eligibility. Must be cooperative, service-oriented, and able to work effectively with frequent interruptions. Must possess excellent interpersonal skills. Must have the ability to exercise good judgment, organize tasks, follow written and verbal directions, and communicate effectively. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job. Unless reasonable accommodations can be made, while performing this job the staff member shall: Use strength to lift items needed to perform the functions of the job. Sit, stand and walk for required periods of time. Speak and hear. Use close vision, color vision, peripheral vision and depth perception along with the ability to focus vision. Communicate effectively in English, using proper grammar and vocabulary. American Sign Language or Braille may also be considered as acceptable forms of communication. Reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers to handle objects and operate tools, technology equipment, computers, and/or controls. WORK PLACE EXPECTATIONS: Work effectively with and respond to diverse cultures or backgrounds. Demonstrate professionalism and appropriate judgment in behavior, speech, and dress in a neat, clean and professional manner for the assignment and work setting. Have regular and punctual attendance. Follow all District policies, work procedures, and reasonable requests by the proper authorities. As an employee of Beaufort County School District, I am to adhere to confidentiality as directed by Federal, State and Local Laws/Regulations; and in accordance with district administrative rules as it pertains to sensitive information, employee information and student information. Failure to adhere to confidentiality may result in disciplinary actions, up to and including termination of employment. REPORTS TO: Athletic Director DAYS PER YEAR: To be Determined SALARY CLASSIFICATION: Paid on the Stipend Schedule EMPLOYEE GROUP: Part-Time Temporary APPLICATION PROCEDURES: Must apply online by completing the appropriate application and adding this specific job to your online application. FLSA STATUS: Non-Exempt EMPLOYEE'S ACKNOWLEDGEMENT STATEMENT: 1. I have reviewed the above position description and understand its contents. 2. I am aware that my position description may be revised or updated at any time and once notified of changes, I remain responsible for knowledge of its contents. 3. I hereby certify that I possess the physical and mental ability to fulfill the essential functions of the above position with or without reasonable accommodation(s). If I require accommodation(s) in order to fulfill any or all of these functions, I agree to provide information to the District regarding the requested accommodation(s). Beaufort County is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION PURPOSE: Responsible for supporting the school-wide literacy/math programs and for providing instruction to students in small and large group settings. The Interventionist uses research and best practices in the teaching of reading, writing, listening, and speaking across the content areas and works directly with students to improve their literacy levels. The Interventionist works with colleagues to identify students who are in need of instructional support. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Uses research-based instruction and data to develop instructional support and progress monitoring plan for students. Provides research based reading interventions(MTSS) to students who need support. Attends the ongoing professional development provided by the school and district. Attends required meetings in current research and practices. Uses student assessment data to guide instruction. Plans student interventions with teachers and Instructional Coaches. Provides intervention and support directly to students reading below grade level. Uses best and effective practices in daily instruction of students. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Must have a valid South Carolina teaching or be able to obtain one within 60 days from being hired. Must be Highly Qualified in the content area of reading or be able to become Highly Qualified in the content area of reading within 60 days from being hired. EXPERIENCE : Must have completed a program of study in education from an accredited college or university. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job. Unless reasonable accommodations can be made, while performing this job the staff member shall: Use strength to lift items needed to perform the functions of the job. Sit, stand and walk for required periods of time. Speak and hear. Use close vision, color vision, peripheral vision and depth perception along with the ability to focus vision. Communicate effectively in English, using proper grammar and vocabulary. American Sign Language or Braille may also be considered as acceptable forms of communication. Reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers to handle objects and operate tools, technology equipment, computers, and/or controls. WORK PLACE EXPECTATIONS: Work effectively with and respond to diverse cultures or backgrounds. Demonstrate professionalism and appropriate judgment in behavior, speech, and dress in a neat, clean and professional manner for the assignment and work setting. Have regular and punctual attendance. Follow all District policies, work procedures, and reasonable requests by the proper authorities. As an employee of Beaufort County School District, I am to adhere to confidentiality as directed by Federal, State and Local Laws/Regulations; and in accordance with district administrative rules as it pertains to sensitive information, employee information and student information. Failure to adhere to confidentiality may result in disciplinary actions, up to and including termination of employment. Attends required meetings in current research and practices. Uses student assessment data to guide instruction. Plans student interventions with teachers and Instructional Coaches. Provides intervention and support directly to students reading below grade level. Uses best and effective practices in daily instruction of students. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Must have a valid South Carolina teaching or be able to obtain one within 60 days from being hired. Must be Highly Qualified in the content area of reading or be able to become Highly Qualified in the content area of reading within 60 days from being hired. EXPERIENCE : Must have completed a program of study in education from an accredited college or university. Beaufort County is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION PURPOSE: The Numeracy Coach will empower teachers by working directly with all stakeholders to bring about improvements in the classroom/school that will positively impact student achievement. The work of the numeracy coach will be based on scientifically based research and content knowledge. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Assist teachers in analyzing data in order to provide differentiation, track trends, support instructional decisions and plan lessons. Work with teachers to create and interpret formative and summative assessments. Plan collaboratively with teachers on a regular basis to insure that: Everyday Mathematics/SC Mathematics Standards are taught with fidelity; paced appropriately Teachers are familiar with the SC Standards and Support Documents Differentiation is occurring in the classroom Increase teachers' awareness of instructional resources and how to use them effectively. Provide professional development for teachers on research based instructional strategies, content and pedagogy; Plan opportunities for horizontal and vertical articulation to occur; provide training on district programs such as Gizmos, Everyday Mathematics, Curriculum Mapping, Number Worlds, graphing calculators Co-teach/model lessons with/for teachers. Serve as a liaison between school and district; collaborate with coaches from other schools. Observe teachers in a coaching capacity to provide feedback and support instruction. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Principal, Chief Instructional Services Officer, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Must have a valid South Carolina Professional teaching certificate or be able to obtain one within 60 days from being hired. Must be Highly Qualified in the content area or be able to become Highly Qualified within 60 days from being hired. Master's degree in Elementary/Middle School or Math preferred; or any equivalent combination of education and experience that provides the required knowledge, skills, and abilities. EXPERIENCE : Three to five years experience as a Math/Science teacher or elementary grade level preferred. Job.JobTitle: 2026-2027 JROTC - Senior Military Instructor at Battery Creek High School
Job.JobDescription:
Beaufort County is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION PURPOSE: Teach, coach and mentor JROTC Cadets to improve their learning and leadership capabilities, and make them better citizens and community leaders, administer and manage the JROTC program effectively according to law, regulations, policies and principles. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Actively recruit students to join JROTC. Achieve desired learning goals by teaching prescribed JROTC subjects listed in the Program of Instruction. Modify and improve given instruction by remaining continuously alert to new and alternative instructional methods and motivational techniques. Maintain supply facilities in strict compliance with individual service security regulations. Comply with service and school district safety guidelines in all instances when instructing, supervising, monitoring, and accompanying or escorting cadets. Maintain student records as prescribed in service regulations and local policies. Prepare unit for formal inspections. Counsel cadets on their performance. Conduct a public affairs program directed at school and community to further enhance understanding of the JROTC program. Supervise/conduct training at JROTC summer camp and other duties prescribed by camp commander. Assist cadets in applying for Senior ROTC scholarships and service academy appointments. Set the appropriate example in decorum and cooperativeness, positive attitude and consistency for cadets and colleagues. Draw, maintain and account for all property issued to the school; ensure sensitive items of equipment are secured. Organize, supervise, and develop extracurricular activities to include color guard, drill teams, and special programs. Maintain and manage all required budgets. Supervise student fund raising activities. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Ability to communicate effectively, both verbal and written. Retired Commissioned Officer for senior instructor or retired Non-Commissioned Officer for assistant instructor. Must be JROTC certified by appropriate military service. EXPERIENCE : Must be retired from the appropriate military service. Exhibits a professional and caring manner to students and parents; efficient, knowledgeable and mission oriented with faculty, administration, and other district personnel. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job. Unless reasonable accommodations can be made, while performing this job the staff member shall: Use strength to lift items needed to perform the functions of the job. Sit, stand and walk for required periods of time. Speak and hear. Use close vision, color vision, peripheral vision and depth perception along with the ability to focus vision. Communicate effectively in English, using proper grammar and vocabulary. American Sign Language or Braille may also be considered as acceptable forms of communication. Reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers to handle objects and operate tools, technology equipment, computers, and/or controls. WORK PLACE EXPECTATIONS: Work effectively with and respond to diverse cultures or backgrounds. Demonstrate professionalism and appropriate judgment in behavior, speech, and dress in a neat, clean and professional manner for the assignment and work setting. Have regular and punctual attendance. Follow all District policies, work procedures, and reasonable requests by the proper authorities. As an employee of Beaufort County School District, I am to adhere to confidentiality as directed by Federal, State and Local Laws/Regulations; and in accordance with district administrative rules as it pertains to sensitive information, employee information and student information. Failure to adhere to confidentiality may result in disciplinary actions, up to and including termination of employment. REPORTS TO: Principal DAYS PER YEAR: 260 SALARY CLASSIFICATION: Determined By The Minimum Instructor Pay (MIP) letter generated by the appropriate military service. Job.JobTitle: ADJUNCT COACH - ASST. BOYS LACROSSE COACH (JOB ID: 26391)
Job.JobDescription:
Riverside High is looking for an Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Summary: Athletic positions require someone with proven decision-making skills, a track record of initiative and effort and a commitment to young people and education. Our jobs require a “people person” that indicates through actions a personality that is conducive to positive interactions with students, staff and parents. To view the job description for each type of athletic coach, click here on the Athletics Position Descriptions. Click here on the Supplements for Athletics link to see athletics supplementary salary schedule. Coaching positions require the following Continuing Education Requirements: GCS Safety Videos Required Annually: · Bloodborne Pathogen Exposure Prevention · FERPA: Confidentiality of Records · GCS – Title IX & Safe Schools · GCS – Code of Ethics & Social Media · GCS – Information Security Mgmt System · GCS – Safety Training · GCS – Mandated Reporter · Greenville Energy Conservation

Paterson Public S.D. is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Effective Date(s): December 5, 2025- March 15, 2026 Staff Needed: One (1) Assistant Coach Cheer Qualifications: New Jersey Teacher's Certificate OR Substitute Teacher Certificate; proof of experience required for substitute certificate holder Coaching experience preferred, but not required Must be available daily during the spring sports season Must be certified in CPR and AED, and holding a current certificate in both Must obtain a "Concussion Awareness and Wellness" certificate or its equivalent, renewed annually Must Obtain a "Heat Acclimatization Awareness and Wellness" certificate or it's equivalent, renewed annually. All new coaches will have one year after being hired to successfully complete a course in "Sports First Aid". Salary: $5,391.00 - $6,532.00 Initiator: MarQuette Burgess, Interim Athletic Supervisor Meet such alternatives to above qualifications as the Superintendent may find appropriate and acceptable Pending budget availability All posting are open for ten (10) consecutive days following the date posted on the Paterson Public Schools website

Job.JobDescription:
Carson City is looking for a Volleyball coach to join our program. Apply now.
Posting ID#: RR-145335 Date: December 2, 2025 POSITION: Girls Volleyball Head Coach LOCATION: Carson High School OPEN TO: Employees of Carson City School District and external applicants. APPLICATION PERIOD: Closes December 15, 2025 or until filled. SALARY: Stipend of 8.0% licensed salary schedule, step one, Master’s Degree column. NOTE: If you are recommended for hire, you will be required to complete the First Aid/AED/CPR course prior to starting this position. If you currently have a valid First Aid/AED/CPR card, please upload a copy of your certificate with your application. APPLICATION: Complete the online application by clicking "Apply for this Position." The application will require: High School coaches are required to have the following documents: A clear understanding of the assignment and its responsibilities, the rules of the sport, knowledge of the Carson City School District Policies and Regulations, as they apply to student activities, including the Code of Ethics; and the ability to develop a self-discipline within participating members that promotes achievement, citizenship and respect. Bi-Lingual preferred. Must be able to pass pre-employment drug screen test. This is a replacement position. REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION: The Human Resources Department will make efforts to provide reasonable accommodations to disabled candidates in the employment process. If you have special needs, please notify the Human Resources Department at the time you turn in your application or at least (3) days prior to an interview by calling (775) 283-2130. Notice of Non-Discrimination: The Carson City School District does not discriminate against any person on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, disability, age, or on any other basis protected by state or federal law, and it provides equal access to the Boy Scouts of America and other designated youth groups. The following person has been designated to handle inquiries regarding the District’s non-discrimination policies: Title IX and 504 Coordinator, 1402 W. King Street, Carson City, NV 89703, (775) 283-2130.

Humboldt County is looking for a Football Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
JOB POSTING SUMMARY DESCRIPTION: Under general supervision of the Principal, the coach/advisor is responsible for advising students of Humboldt County School District in a specified athletic or non-athletic Extra Duty Activity, requiring a high standard of sound and acceptable coaching/advising practices. The advisor adheres to a highly efficient and technically sound program of injury prevention and safety precautions and performs related work as required. EXEMPLARY DUTIES/RESPONSIBILITIES: The coach/advisor is responsible for a variety of tasks related to leadership, student welfare, finance, and administration: Leadership and Student Welfare Develops rapport with students and provides sound leadership. Is responsible for being an example of good sportsmanship. Applies discipline in a fair and positive manner and according to prescribed procedures. Is responsible for the general health and welfare of students and for the actions and conduct of students. Work with student body to improve student outcomes. Communication and Public Relations Maintains cooperation with Principal and other Extra Duty personnel. Maintains good communications with the Principal by keeping them informed regarding the overall program, including trips, games, etc.. Develops good public relations in the school and community. Administrative and Financial Management Hold scheduled meetings. Create monthly agendas, meeting minutes, and sign-in sheets. Complete fundraising request forms. Follow accounting and purchasing procedures with bookkeeper. Raise funds for class in coordination with administration and other clubs. Supervise community service projects and fundraisers for the school. Facilities and Operations Maintain and clean concessions areas and equipment. Follow health inspector guidelines. General Performs other duties as assigned.

Job.JobTitle: HS Asst Cheerleading Coach - 2026-27 school year (Football/Basketball)
Job.JobDescription:
Perrysburg is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer coach to join our program. Apply now.
General Description: To prepare a sport team for interscholastic competition.
Essential Functions: 1. Encourage students to participate in the program. 2. Account for the condition, inventory, distribution and procurement of all equipment. 3. Account for the procurement and distribution of all medical supplies and equipment. 4. Account for assignment of lockers and the condition of the locker room. 5. Schedule, record and assist as needed with physical examinations for all players. 6. Account for the collection of all fees. 7. Instruct and assist players in the prevention and care of injuries. 8. Accountable for all players meeting necessary requirements pertaining to insurance, physical cards, eligibility forms and athletic waivers. 9. Attend rules interpretation meetings. 10. Assist in the preparation of facilities for all games and practice sessions. 11. Schedule, plan and conduct all practice sessions and scrimmages. 12. Accountable for player behavior during practice sessions, meetings, locker room activities, bus trips and other activities under the support of the program. 13. Schedule and conduct meetings to discuss forthcoming opponents. 14. Support the athletic program as a whole. 15. Delegate specific duties, supervise implementation and, at season's end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluate all assistant coaches. 16. Contribute to public relations by providing information to newspapers, radios and television stations, school newspapers and other school publications. 17. Correspond with college and university personnel to assist high school players in acquiring athletic scholarships. 18. Communicate with classroom teachers to determine the achievement level of each player. 19. Maintain a record of team statistics and requirements for lettering or certificates of award. 20. Participate in Awards Program by distributing awards to the players.
Other Duties and Responsibilities: 1. Serve as a role model for students in how to conduct themselves as citizens and as responsible, intelligent human beings. 2. Help instill in students the belief in and practice of sportsmanship, ethics and integrity, as it pertains to the Board of Education Adopted Policy 2431B – Sportsmanship Expectations. 3. Conduct other duties related to the coach's duties as assigned by the Athletic Director and/or Principal.
The employee shall remain free of any alcohol or nonprescribed controlled substance abuse in the workplace throughout his/her employment in the District. The Perrysburg Exempted Village School District Board of Education is an equal opportunity employer offering employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, national origin, age or disability. This job description summary does not imply that these are the only duties to be performed. This job description is subject to change.

Job.JobDescription:
South-Western City School District is looking for a Weightlifting Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
The Big Walnut Local School District is seeking a Strength and Fitness Coach. We seek an individual who is organized, energetic, and self - motivated. The successful candidate must demonstrate strong interpersonal and verbal communication skills with the ability to develop and maintain effective relationships, excel in a team environment. Applicants are encouraged to submit completed application no later than February 16, 2026. Job Goals: To improve performance of athletes by developing a comprehensive speed, strength, and agility program for grades 7 - 12 To reduce athletic injuries by developing sport-specific training To provide nutritional guidelines to student athletes To provide weight room training to coaches To provide ongoing training to District employees to increase wellness Minimum Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Exercise Science or related field Strong relationship and communication skills General knowledge of Google Must provide current and clear BCI/FBI background checks PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Collaborate with coaches and athletic administration to create a mission and vision for the Big Walnut Athletic Strength and Agility Program Develop sport-specific systematic training for teams and individual athlete, while working closely with the BW athletic administration and coaching staff - including proper lifting/training techniques, creating training programs, supervise training and assessing performance Develop team and individual training goals, provide feedback to athletes, coaches and athletic administration Attend pre-season parent meetings to discuss the Big Walnut Athletic Strength and Agility Program Attend Big Walnut athletics professional development sessions Ensure cleanliness, organization, and maintenance of weight room Communicate on a regular basis with athletic administration Take personal responsibility for ongoing professional development to keep abreast of changing concepts in the field Maintain ethical and professional behavior at all times, serving as a positive role model Able to handle confidential and sensitive information Display a positive, respectful demeanor with students, staff, and community members Other duties as assigned Salary: 260-day contract Salary commensurate with experience For more information, please contact Brian Shelton, BWHS Athletic Director (brianshelton@bwls.net).

Job.JobTitle: Head Varsity Volleyball Coach, High School
Job.JobDescription:
South-Western City School District is looking for a Volleyball Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Head Varsity Volleyball Coach Northridge High School is seeking a dynamic and student-centered leader to serve as Head Varsity Volleyball Coach for the 2026 school year. Northridge takes pride in its athletic tradition, strong community support, and commitment to excellence both in competition and the classroom. About Northridge Local Schools Northridge Local Schools serves students in Johnstown and surrounding areas in Licking County. The district is known for its supportive community, commitment to academic excellence, and strong extracurricular programs. Located northeast of Columbus, Johnstown offers a close-knit community atmosphere with access to the opportunities of the greater Columbus area. Responsibilities Provide leadership for the entire volleyball program (712) Develop and implement a comprehensive program focused on skill development and team culture Promote academic achievement, sportsmanship, and character development Ensure compliance with all district and OHSAA regulations Communicate effectively with players, parents, staff, and the community Qualifications Previous coaching experience preferred Strong leadership and organizational skills Valid Pupil Activity Permit (or ability to obtain) Completion of required certifications and background checks Application Process Interested candidates must complete an application and submit a resume to: Kevin Jarrett Athletic Director kjarrett@northridgevikings.org This position will remain open until filled; however, applications must be submitted no later than March 9, 2026 .

Job.JobTitle: JV and Varsity Ast Boys Soccer Coach - 2 positions
Job.JobDescription:
South-Western City School District is looking for a Soccer Assistant JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
JV BOYS SOCCER - LEVEL C AST BOYS SOCCER - LEVEL C JONATHAN ALDER LOCAL SCHOOLS TITLE: Assistant Athletic Coach - supplemental position SUGGESTED QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Valid driver's license. 2. State of Ohio Teaching License (preferred, not required). 3. Coaching and administrative abilities. 4. Experience in related field 5. Required to maintain a valid Pupil Activity Permit Validation and all items required to obtain PAP via the Ohio Department of Education. Copies of all certifications must be on file prior to coaching. REPORTS TO: Head Coach, Athletic Director, Principal and/or Designee SALARY: Supplemental as per negotiated agreement POSITION GOAL: To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self discipline, self confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. To upgrade his/her knowledge and skills through coaching clinics, observations, consultation, etc. SUPERVISES: The assistant coach must advise, coordinate and support a staff of coaches at the high school and middle school level in conjunction with the athletic director and respective principal. 1.0 PROFESSIONAL & PERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS 1.01 Assists the head coach in regard to submitting participant lists, bus times, parent permission, physical cards, year-end reports and program information relative to your sport. 1.02 Follows policy in the Athletic Handbook and meets all criteria as outlined in the job description. 1.03 Provides training rules to team members in writing and follows due process procedures. 1.04 Develops rapport with the athletic coaching staff. 1.05 Participates in in-service meetings and other activities to improve coaching performance. Attends meetings necessary to the welfare of the Athletic Department. 1.06 Develops sound public relations. Co-operates with newspapers, radio, T.V., Booster Club, and interested spectators. Upholds the integrity of the athletic program by refraining from making negative remarks. 1.07 Maintains suitable sideline conduct at games. Expects sportsmanlike behavior to be displayed by assistant coaches and athletes toward all individuals involved with the contest (officials, workers, fans, etc.). 1.08 Serves as a role model to players, coaches and spectators in demonstrating good sportsmanship and ethical behavior at all times. 1.09 Demonstrates respect for opponents before, during and after the contest. Displays modesty in victory and graciousness in defeat. 1.10 Works cooperatively with junior high coaches in developing a coordinated program. 1.11 Works cooperatively with Head Coach and Athletic Director and completes duties assigned to them 1.12 Attends all meetings called by the Athletic Director. 2. COACHING PERFORMANCE 2.01 Develops respect by example in appearance, manners, behavior, language, and sportsmanlike conduct during a contest. 2.02 Is well-versed and knowledgeable in matters pertaining to the sport. 2.03 Has individual and team discipline and control. 2.04 Is fair, understanding, tolerant, sympathetic and patient with team members. 2.05 Educates players on rules and regulations of the game. Expects players to follow them and to accept the decisions about the game made my officials. 2.06 Teaches sportsmanship and rewards players who are good sports. 2.07 Teaching ability is innovative using new coaching techniques and ideas in addition to sound, already proven methods of coaching. 2.08 Is prompt in meeting team for practices and games. 2.09 Shows an interest in athletes in off-season activities and classroom efforts. 2.10 Delegates authority with responsibility while remaining accountable for such delegations. 2.11 Team performance consistent with quality of athletes available. 2.12 Supervision of players in the locker room, practice, field, bus and other areas. 3. RELATED COACHING RESPONSIBILITIES 3.01 Is concerned about the care of equipment, including issue, collection, inventory and storage. 3.02 Is cooperative in preparation of non-league scheduling. 3.03 Is cooperative in helping service clubs, Booster Club, Recreation Department and other organizations in their projects, which in turn relate to our athletic program. 3.04 Follows proper procedure for purchase of equipment. 3.05 Operates sport within the budget as submitted.

Job.JobDescription:
Hamilton High School - Main Campus is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Athletics/Activities to join our program. Apply now.
DANCE TEAM ADVISOR Job Category: Supplemental Extra-curricular Position Job Location: Hamilton High School Job Type: Part-time When to Apply: ASAP Qualifications: Dance Team Advisor Middle School/High School Hamilton City School District Job Goal: T he dance coach/advisor is the leader and mentor for competitive dancers. The advisor will design/choreograph unique dance sequences and teach these routines to team members (students). The advisor will ensure that dance routines are polished and perfected for public performances. Reports to: Director of Fine Arts Essential Functions: The dance team advisor will: Serve as a role model for students Encourage and inspire all students to grow, develop and be successful Train and teach students the concept of professional standards for performance Provide learning experiences for students at levels of learning Ensure that students are learning and developing in their ability to dance Establish an effective rehearsal schedule and communicate the schedule with parents and students Establish procedures that address all safety concerns Train team members in the areas of safety and care for each other Explicitly teach and ensure professional standards for student performances Embrace and teach the philosophy of working together and contributing to a purpose larger than oneself Create a program that allows self-expression through the art of dance Inspire student team members to aspire for excellence Lead student dancers to develop artistry by honing their technique and skills Lay the foundation for life-long skills of commitment, resilience, and responsibility Promote and cultivate a forward-thinking, positive mindset Carefully monitor team progress to ensure individual and team success Be attuned to the mental health of their students and know when they need to push a student to attain greater learning or to back off when a student is resistant Teach warm-up and cool-down exercises and routines that are in line with current teaching practices Choreograph creative routines that build and showcase student talent and student learning Showcase the dance team (perform) at Football Games, Basketball Games and Community Events Use positive and inclusive language when addressing their team Understand and know the difference between using constructive criticism rather than giving unhelpful opinions and judgments Continually update their own skills by taking classes and attending courses that enhance their teaching and benefit their students and the overall performance Communicate well with their community of clients: students, parents, band director(s), athletic director Qualifications Profile : Strong background in the performing arts, including dance experience in hip hop, jazz, Broadway, modern dance, and other appropriate forms. High-quality oral and written communication skills Strong organizational and planning skills Open to new ideas and the ability to adapt to change Awareness of, and appreciation for, individual uniqueness and diversity Awareness of safety concerns and risk management Pupil Activity Permit, CPR, and First Aid certifications required Good understanding of dance pedagogy and movement anatomy Knowledgeable about stage production and costume design Minimum of High School Diploma or equivalent Physical Requirements: The physical and mental demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this position. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. Ability to drive an automobile. Ability to travel freely throughout the building Ability to lift, carry or move equipment or supplies weighing over one-hundred pounds Ability to manage balancing, bending, climbing, crouching, kneeling, reaching, or standing Ability to communicate effectively using language and writing skills Ability to participate in and facilitate the exchange of ideas leading to a group consensus Ability to react productively to frequent interruptions and changing conditions Ability to analyze data and present alternative actions and solutions Ability to set priorities, organize and complete duties efficiently without supervision Ability to maintain a tactful disposition in dealing with administrators, staff, students, parents, and the public Responsibility for the safety or well-being of other people Conduct: Each staff member shall remain free of any alcohol or non-prescribed controlled substance and abuse of any prescribed controlled substance in the workplace throughout his/her employment in the district. (Licensure Code of Professional Conduct) Each staff member shall serve as a role model for students in how to conduct themselves as citizens and as responsible, intelligent human beings. Each staff member has a moral responsibility to help instill in students the belief in and practice of ethical principles and democratic values . Terms of Employment : Per the Hamilton City Schools Board of Education/Hamilton Classroom Teachers Association Negotiated Agreement The Hamilton City School District is an Equal Opportunity Employer and as such does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, sex, gender identification, sexual orientation, age, religion, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, marital status, or any other status protected by law.

Job.JobDescription:
Hamilton High School - Main Campus is looking for a Volleyball Athletics/Activities to join our program. Apply now.
VOLLEYBALL, GIRLS VARSITY ASSISTANT COACH Starting Date: AUGUST 1, 2026 Job Category: Supplemental Extra-Curricular Position Job Location: Hamilton High School Job Type: Part-time When to Apply: February 26, 2026 UNTIL FILLED Salary: Per Supplemental Salary Schedule in Master Contract (Rate Group 3) Qualifications: Should possess a strong background in coaching Volleyball. Job.JobTitle: Behavioral Intervention Coach - Anticipated for the 2026-2027 School Year
Job.JobDescription:
Hamilton City School District is looking for a Student Support Services to join our program. Apply now.
Employment Opportunity Butler County Educational Service Center ANTICIPATED FOR THE 2026-2027 SCHOOL YEAR POSITION: Behavioral Intervention Coach STARTING DATE: August 2026 SALARY: Based on the approved Governing Board Salary Schedule WORK YEAR: Full Time DEADLINE: Until Filled GENERAL DESCRIPTION: The behavioral intervention coach will provide educational coaching to teachers of contracted districts in the areas of specialized instruction, comprehensive curriculum design, classroom management, behavioral intervention, academic supports, strategies and interventions for students identified with autism spectrum disorder, other low incidence disabilities as well as those identified students that are having significant behavioral difficulties within the classroom environment. QUALIFICATIONS: A Bachelor's degree required and holds a valid Ohio Department of Education license with coursework in the education of special education and or a board-certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA). Extensive knowledge of special education required as well as behavioral interventions. Three years successful teaching experience and working with special education students including those that exhibit intensive behavioral needs. Demonstrated knowledge of regulatory standards and district policies related to assigned functions. Demonstrated knowledge of Special Education as well as restraint and seclusion policies and regulations. Good organizational skills. Interpersonal skills necessary to communicate effectively and work productively with all levels of district staff, students, parents and the general public. Effective verbal and written communication skills and able to handle problem situations in a tactful, courteous and respectful manner. Ability to analyze and summarize data related to assessment results and develops plans of action to address a variety of behavioral issues and concerns. Skills necessary to provide guidance, training, modeling of behavioral practices and interventions. Ability to adapt and respond to multiple priorities and demands, deal with the concerns of staff/students/parents in a timely manner, and resolve problem situations in a positive manner. Willingness/ability to work with students and parents as a team member. Computer expertise appropriate to position. Valid Ohio driver's license and access to personal vehicle. History of good job attendance. Must pass criminal background checks, drug screening, and be free from communicable disease. The Butler County Educational Service Center is an equal opportunity employer. Applications will be accepted without regard to age, race, handicap, color, sex or national origin. The regulations of Title VI, VII (Civil Rights Acts of 1984), Equal Pay act of 1963, Americans With Disabilities Act, Title IX, and the Drug free Schools and Communities Act of 1989 are followed. (If you need an accommodation to interview, contact Human Resources by the deadline.

Job.JobTitle: Middle School 7th Grade Basketball Cheer Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Beavercreek City School District is looking for an Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Applications are now being accepted for a Middle School 7th Grade Basketball Cheer Coach- Winter- Scale 10 Salary range of $1,257.00 - $2,614.00 to be paid in 2 pays 2026-2027 School Year All applicants must possess a valid Ohio Certificate/License/Permit in the appropriate area. Proper athletic certification is required for this assignment, if hired. These can be submitted with your application if complete. Applicants who are not yet certified may apply. Coaches' Tool Chest - https://coachestoolchest.com/ - $49 fee; will certify you in all needed areas including Fundamentals of Coaching, First Aid for Coaches, Concussion Training, CPR Training, and Sudden Cardiac Arrest Training. This will need to be renewed yearly. Apply for or submit a Pupil Activity Permit (PAP) - through the Ohio Department of Education. Price varies depending on the length of permit you qualify for; typically a 3 or 5 year permit. If you do not have an OH-ID login, one must be created for initial access. For more details, please visit the ODE website: https://education.ohio.gov/Topics/Teaching/Licensure/Audiences/Coaching-Permits Complete a background check if hired - through Beavercreek City Schools board office - $47.25 fee; cash, check or charge. This can be scheduled anytime by clicking here: Background Check Calendar . 4029 Executive Drive, Beavercreek, OH 45430. Thank you for your interest! Beavercreek City Schools Athletic Department
Job.CompanyDescription: Beavercreek City School District coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Beavercreek City School District is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Winter Sideline Cheer Head Coach SPRINGBORO HIGH SCHOOL SUMMARY: This person will be employed on a supplemental contract and be assigned duties of head winter sideline cheerleading advisor for High School activities. Reports to Athletic Director. DUTIES AND PERFORMANCE EXPECTATIONS: Supervisory responsibilities for practice sessions and scheduled home and away games. Organize and set all practice sessions. Distribute, collect and maintain an inventory of all equipment and uniforms. Keep all necessary records and forms. Cooperate with all concerned in the athletic programs at Springboro. Maintain discipline at all practice sessions and games. Promote school and student pride by requiring cheerleaders to behave in an appropriate and respectful manner when dealing with officials, players, coaches, spectators, and school personnel. Promote school and student pride by requiring cheerleaders to wear dress clothes or uniforms on game days. Arrange for and conduct selection procedures and try-outs for the following year's squad. Assure squads adherence to cheerleading rules and regulations. Perform other functions as necessary to implement goals and objectives of the wrestling and athletic programs at Springboro. Aid/oversee cheerleading competitions as necessary. Aid in coordinating pep assemblies. Aid/oversee planning, directing and organization of cheerleading clinics. Perform other duties as assigned. QUALIFICATIONS : Valid teaching certificate/license (preferred) Prior supervisory experience and/or knowledge of cheerleading preferred. Ability to work with young students and provide a positive influence on their lives. Maintain current Pupil Activity Supervisor validation and CPR training certificate as required by Board policy and the Ohio Department of Education. Must pass criminal background check. CONDITIONS OF EMPLOYMENT: Effective Date: 2026/2027 school year Length of Employment: One-year supplemental contract. Salary: According to the negotiated agreement.

Job.JobDescription:
Delhi Middle School is looking for a Cross Country Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
OAK HILLS LOCAL SCHOOL DISTRICT JOB DESCRIPTION JOBTITLE: HEAD COACH REPORTS TO: Athletic Director JOBOBJECTIVE: Coaches assigned student athletic activity. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: Sport specific coaching skills substantiated by training/work experience. Must possess the ability to organize and direct a multi-level program within the assigned sport. Holds or is qualified to obtain a Pupil Activity Supervisor Permit/CPR/NFHS Coaches Education. Complies with drug-free workplace rules, board policies and administrative guidelines/procedures. Demonstrates knowledge of current Ohio High School Athletic Association regulations and by-laws. Duties require working irregular hours and/or a non-traditional schedule. Embodies high ethical standards/integrity. Accepts personal responsibility for decisions/conduct. Exhibits consistent, impartial and resourceful critical-thinking skills. Maintains a record free of criminal violations that would prohibit public school employment. Position may require an Ohio School Van Driver Certificate as a condition of employment. NOTE: Supplemental contracts are offered to licensed staff with appropriate training, knowledge and experience. Unfilled positions may be offered to other qualified applicants meeting school district and state department education criteria. Verification of qualifications may be required (i.e., official academic transcripts/diplomas, previous employers, professional references, etc.). ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Instructs student athletes in physical conditioning, tactics and sportsmanship. Provides direction, support and accountability to help students benefit from program participation. Reviews procedures/schedules with students and parents before the start of the season. Communicates program objectives/performance expectations to stakeholders. Attends mandatory programs as outlined in the Coaches Handbook. Conducts a risk assessment. Implements safety precautions. Responds to emergencies. Helps supervise the preparation of athletic fields and practice/training areas as directed. Confirms equipment is appropriate for participants' physical development and skill level. Assists with participant uniforms (e.g. ordering, distribution, collection, refurbishing, etc.). Keeps track of school equipment assigned to students. Plans/directs program activities (e.g., practice schedules, training clinics, etc.). Keeps administrators informed about emerging issues. Trains assistant coaches in procedures to accomplish duties effectively. Assumes responsibility for the results of duties delegated to assigned staff. Qualifies students for participation. Confirms medical/scholastic eligibility. Informs students about personal responsibilities. Verifies all required medical authorizations forms are on file and readily available. Teaches safety measures to prevent injuries Consults with parents as needed (e.g., phone calls, E-mail, written notes, meetings, etc.). Ensures all athletes receive appropriate instruction, support and opportunities to participate. Evaluates individual/team performance. Prepares statistics. Develops/refines game strategies. Recruits, trains and supervises student managers, trainers and scouts. Promotes fair play. Demonstrates respect for rules, officials, visiting teams, and the public. Facilitates participation in board-approved local, regional and/or national competitions. Arranges transportation and accompanies participants to/from sanctioned athletic activities. Promotes respect for program participants. Plans/attends recognition events. Verifies participants have fulfilled all requirements for letters, awards and/or participation certificates. Ensures that program activities are self-sustaining except when authorized by the administration. Attends financial in-service training to keep current with accounting procedures as directed. Supervises approved fund raising projects. Works with the athletic director to ensure that all financial activities are processed through the proper student activity account. Exemplifies professionalism and fosters goodwill to enhance the district's public image. Contributes to an effective and positive work/learning environment. Completes all assigned duties. Develops mutually respectful relationships with co-workers. Functions as part of a cohesive team. Encourages community participation in school-sponsored activities. Helps students understand/embrace ethical conduct and democratic values. Maintains a professional appearance. Wears work attire appropriate for the position. Maintains an acceptable attendance record and is punctual. Respects privacy and maintains the confidentiality of privileged information. Strives to develop rapport and serve as a positive role model for others. Maintains open/effective communications. Serves as an information resource. Actively participates in staff meetings, conferences and other required school activities. Prepares/maintains accurate records. Submits required paperwork on time. Provides prompt notification of personal delays or absences. Refers district policy interpretation questions to an appropriate administrator. Seeks clarification when directives/expectations are unclear. Uses active listening and problem-solving techniques to resolve questions/concerns tactfully. Uses diplomacy/self-control when dealing with other individuals. Respects diversity. Pursues opportunities to enhance professional performance. Keeps current with advances in technology associated with work assignments. Maintains applicable credentials. Complies with all state licensure requirements. Works toward mastery of individualized development/performance goals as directed. Takes precautions to ensure safety. Helps manage/eliminate risks. Intervenes to prevent/stop bullying and inappropriate student behavior. Provides appropriate supervision. Implements effective pupil management procedures. Upholds the athletic conduct code. Maintains high expectations for acceptable student behavior. Reports suspected child abuse/neglect to civil authorities as required by law. Watches for situations that may indicate a problem. Performs other specific job-related duties as directed. Assists with unexpected/urgent situations as needed. Helps implement workplace initiatives that advance district goals. WORKING CONDITIONS: Safety is essential to job performance. Employees must exercise caution and comply with standard safety regulations and district procedures when involved in the following situations: Balancing, bending, climbing, crouching, kneeling, reaching, or standing. Exposure to adverse weather conditions and temperature extremes. Exposure to air-borne particulates, chemical irritants, combustible materials, electrical hazards, equipment vibrations, loud sounds, moving mechanical parts and odors. Exposure to wet and/or slippery surfaces. Exposure to blood-borne pathogens and communicable diseases. Interacting with aggressive, disruptive and/or unruly individuals. Lifting, carrying and moving work-related supplies/equipment. Operating and/or riding in a vehicle. Traveling to meetings and work assignments. Performance Evaluation: Job performance is evaluated according to policy provisions and contractual agreements adopted by the Oak Hills Local School District. The Oak Hills Local School District is an equal opportunity employer. This job description identifies primary responsibilities and is not intended to be a complete list of all duties performed. This document is subject to change in response to student demographics, staffing, funding variables, modified operating procedures, program/curriculum changes and unforeseen events. Rev. 11/2011
Job.CompanyDescription: Oak Hills Local School District coaching jobs

Middletown High School is looking for a Basketball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Boys Head Varsity Basketball Coach Qualifications: 1. Previous head coaching experienced preferred. 2. High moral character, proper role model for the students and the community, integrity, and effectively communicates well with players, parents, community members, coaches, and school officials. 3. Ability to organize, supervise, and give direction to a sports team/program. 4. Previous experience as a coach. 5. Knowledge of the technical and fundamental aspects of the assigned sport. A commitment and willingness to continue to learn and examine new ideas pertinent to the assigned sport. Proper working knowledge of rules and regulations established by the league, the school, Ohio High School Athletic Association, and the Board of Education. 6. Holds a valid and proper licensure and certification(s) defined in accordance with the Ohio Department of Education, the Ohio High School Athletic Association, and the Middletown City School District. 7. Acceptable BCII report and FBI report. 8. Ability to meet all job expectations and objectives. 9. Such alternatives to the above qualifications as the Superintendent and/or Board of Education may find appropriate. Please see attached job description for more information. If interested please apply and email Joseph Campolongo, Athletic Director, at jcampolongo@middletowncityschools.com
